detail: [{"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:672-709", "category": "module_boundary_violation", "description": "TestEdgeFetchGuardAssertionStyle is a meta-test that uses inspect.getsource() to introspect the literal source text of another test method (test_force_dry_run_does_not_call_get_all_valid_edges) to verify assertion style. Tests should verify production-code behaviour; tests-that-test-tests belong to static analysis or linters, not the runtime test suite. This blurs the module boundary between production verification and code-quality tooling.", "suggested_fix": "Remove TestEdgeFetchGuardAssertionStyle. The underlying correctness constraint \u2014 that AsyncMock edge-fetch assertions must use assert_not_awaited() \u2014 is already enforced by the direct fix on line 306. If the style rule needs automated enforcement going forward, encode it as a flake8/pylint custom rule or a pre-commit grep check rather than a runtime pytest class.", "reviewer": "reviewer_architect_reviewer"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:682-709", "category": "fragile_coupling", "description": "The meta-test couples to the exact string literals 'backend.get_all_valid_edges.assert_not_awaited()' and 'backend.get_all_valid_edges.assert_not_called()' inside the source of the target test. Any legitimate refactoring of test_force_dry_run_does_not_call_get_all_valid_edges \u2014 renaming the local variable from 'backend', extracting the assertion to a helper, or auto-formatting \u2014 will silently break or erroneously fail the meta-test even if the AsyncMock semantics are preserved.", "suggested_fix": "If meta-level assertion-style checking is truly required, use AST analysis (ast.parse + ast.walk) rather than text-string matching. This survives whitespace changes and variable renames while still detecting the correct method call. Better still, eliminate the meta-test entirely as noted above.", "reviewer": "reviewer_architect_reviewer"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:674", "category": "misleading_naming", "description": "The class is named TestEdgeFetchGuardAssertionStyle, using 'Style' to describe what is actually a semantic correctness constraint (AsyncMock must be awaited, not merely called). 'Style' implies a cosmetic/formatting preference, which understates the intent and may lead future reviewers to dismiss it as optional. The underlying concern \u2014 that assert_not_called() does not verify await behaviour on an AsyncMock \u2014 is a correctness issue, not a style issue.", "suggested_fix": "If the meta-test is retained, rename it to something that conveys its intent, e.g. TestAsyncMockAssertionCorrectness or TestEdgeFetchGuardUsesAwaitedAssertion.", "reviewer": "reviewer_architect_reviewer"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:672-709", "category": "pattern_inconsistency", "description": "inspect.getsource() is used in exactly one place across the entire test suite (confirmed by search). Introducing a one-off introspection pattern for a single assertion style fix creates an orphan idiom that will confuse maintainers who encounter it later. The rest of the file follows a consistent pattern of testing production-code behavior through AsyncMock fixtures.", "suggested_fix": "Keep the test file's pattern consistent: behavioural tests only. The fix to assert_not_awaited() (line 306) is sufficient. No meta-test is needed.", "reviewer": "reviewer_architect_reviewer"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:681-709", "category": "over_engineering", "description": "TestEdgeFetchGuardAssertionStyle uses inspect.getsource() to verify the assertion style of an adjacent test \u2014 a pattern with no precedent anywhere in the test suite (grep confirms this is the only inspect.getsource() call across all tests). The actual fix (changing assert_not_called() to assert_not_awaited() on line 306) is already committed in this same diff. The meta-test adds a novel source-inspection technique, a new import, and 38 lines to guard a one-line regression that any normal test run would immediately surface if reverted. The existing docstring meta-test (TestDocstringAccuracyForceDryRunClass) achieves its goal via __doc__ directly \u2014 no import needed. This new class introduces a heavier, non-idiomatic technique for a problem already solved by the target test itself.", "suggested_fix": "Remove TestEdgeFetchGuardAssertionStyle and the `import inspect` line. The correct assertion is already in place on line 306. If a style regression guard is truly desired, a simple comment on line 306 (e.g. '# AsyncMock: use assert_not_awaited, not assert_not_called') is sufficient and costs nothing. Alternatively, a project-wide linting rule (e.g. a custom flake8/pylint check) would enforce this across all AsyncMock usages rather than guarding one specific call site.", "reviewer": "reviewer_reuse_auditor"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:696", "category": "test_fragility", "description": "`inspect.getsource()` requires access to the source file on disk. In environments where tests are run from compiled bytecode (`.pyc`-only distributions, some CI caching setups, zipimport) it raises `OSError: could not get source code`. This would surface as an unexpected error rather than a meaningful test failure, making CI harder to diagnose.", "suggested_fix": "Wrap the call in a try/except OSError and either skip with `pytest.skip('source unavailable')` or document the requirement that source files must be present. Alternatively, drop the meta-test and rely on the now-correct line 306 directly.", "reviewer": "reviewer_test_analyst"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:699-708", "category": "test_correctness", "description": "The string-presence checks in assertions (a) and (b) scan raw source text, not AST nodes. They will produce false results if the target strings appear in comments or string literals. For example, a comment `# do NOT use backend.get_all_valid_edges.assert_not_called()` inside the target function would cause assertion (b) to fail spuriously. Conversely, `assert_not_awaited()` appearing only in a comment would make assertion (a) pass even if the real assertion was deleted.", "suggested_fix": "If keeping the meta-test, parse the source with `ast.parse` and walk the AST to check for actual `assert_not_awaited` call nodes rather than substring matching. Or remove the meta-test \u2014 the substantive fix is already captured by line 306 and the adjacent `_rebuild_entity_from_edges.assert_not_awaited()` assertion.", "reviewer": "reviewer_test_analyst"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:681-708", "category": "test_value", "description": "`TestEdgeFetchGuardAssertionStyle` is a meta-test that verifies the *text* of another test, not the behavior of any production code. After this diff lands, both its assertions are trivially satisfied by the very fix introduced on line 306. The meta-test adds no coverage of `rebuild_entity_summaries` or any other production path; it only guards against someone reverting line 306 while leaving the meta-test intact \u2014 an unlikely scenario that static linting or code review would catch more cheaply.", "suggested_fix": "Remove `TestEdgeFetchGuardAssertionStyle`. The semantic correctness of using `assert_not_awaited()` over `assert_not_called()` for an `AsyncMock` is already validated behaviorally by the fact that the test at line 292 passes: if `get_all_valid_edges` were awaited when it shouldn't be, `assert_not_awaited()` would raise, whereas `assert_not_called()` would not (since calling and awaiting are separate events on an AsyncMock). The meta-test therefore provides redundant, weaker coverage.", "reviewer": "reviewer_test_analyst"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:292-307", "category": "incomplete_assertion", "description": "`test_force_dry_run_does_not_call_get_all_valid_edges` correctly asserts that `get_all_valid_edges` is not awaited, but does not assert that `list_entity_nodes` *was* awaited (i.e., that the function actually ran through the entity-listing step before short-circuiting). Without this, a trivial no-op implementation of `rebuild_entity_summaries` would pass the test.", "suggested_fix": "Add `backend.list_entity_nodes.assert_awaited_once()` after the existing assertions to confirm the function reached the entity-listing phase before the dry_run guard short-circuited the rebuild.", "reviewer": "reviewer_test_analyst"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:697", "category": "unhandled_exception_at_boundary", "description": "`inspect.getsource(fn)` raises `OSError` (formerly `IOError`) when the source cannot be retrieved \u2014 e.g., if the .py file is absent, the test suite is run from a compiled-only distribution, or the file is inside a zip/egg. This exception is not caught, so the meta-test would fail with an opaque OSError rather than a useful assertion message.", "suggested_fix": "Wrap the call in a try/except and re-raise with a descriptive message: `try:\\n    src = inspect.getsource(fn)\\nexcept OSError as exc:\\n    pytest.fail(f'Could not retrieve source for introspection: {exc}')`", "reviewer": "reviewer_robustness"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py:700-711", "category": "fragile_string_matching", "description": "The meta-test uses exact substring matching (`'backend.get_all_valid_edges.assert_not_awaited()' in src`) on live source code. Any whitespace-only reformatting, line continuation, or auto-formatter change (e.g., Black splitting a long call across lines) will silently break both assertions without changing the semantic behaviour being tested. This makes the test brittle to cosmetic edits.", "suggested_fix": "Normalise the extracted source before matching by collapsing whitespace: `normalised = ' '.join(src.split())`, then assert against the normalised string. Alternatively, use a regex with `\\s*` between tokens to tolerate formatting variations.", "reviewer": "reviewer_robustness"}]
resolution: Pre-triaged review suggestions handled: created 2 follow-up tasks (#571 Remove TestEdgeFetchGuardAssertionStyle meta-test, #572 Add positive list_entity_nodes.assert_awaited_once() assertion) targeting fused-memory/tests/test_graphiti_quality_433.py, and wrote 3 conventions to preferences_and_norms (no runtime meta-tests introspecting test source, prefer await-aware AsyncMock assertions, pair negative guard assertions with at least one positive assertion). 1 suggestion skipped (rename of class slated for deletion — moot).
